title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.title: Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Feast
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Middle Eastern cuisine with our authentic Lamb Ouzi recipe. Learn to slow roast a whole lamb to perfection for a flavorful, show-stopping feast.
longTailKeywords: authentic Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi recipe
lowCompetitionKeywords: whole roasted lamb ouzi with rice
seo.h1: Authentic Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Delight
h2Headings: Pro Tips for Perfect Slow Roasted Lamb
seo.imageAlt: A beautifully presented whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi with rice, nuts, and spices, ready for a Middle Eastern feast.
seo.imageCaption: Impress your guests with this show-stopping whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi, a centerpiece of Middle Eastern hospitality.

description: Lamb Ouzi is a majestic Middle Eastern dish, featuring tender, slow-roasted lamb served over aromatic spiced rice, often adorned with toasted nuts and fresh herbs. This celebratory feast brings families together, offering layers of rich flavor and a truly unforgettable dining experience. Its preparation, while requiring patience, rewards with a deeply savory and fragrant centerpiece perfect for any special occasion.

instructions.title: Prepare the Lamb Marinade
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine olive oil, salt, black pepper, allspice, cinnamon, cardamom, nutmeg, and minced garlic. Mix well to form a thick paste. Score the lamb leg or shoulder deeply in several places with a sharp knife.

instructions.title: Marinate and Sear the Lamb
instructions.text: Rub the spice paste generously all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into the scored cuts. Let it marinate at room temperature for at least 30 minutes, or ideally, refrigerate for 4-6 hours or overnight. Preheat a large oven-safe pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the lamb on all sides until deeply browned, about 3-4 minutes per side. Remove the lamb and set aside.

instructions.title: Slow Roast the Lamb
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 325F (160C). Return the seared lamb to the pot. Add the chopped onion around the lamb. Pour 1 cup of broth into the pot. Cover tightly with a lid or aluminum foil and roast in the preheated oven for 3 to 3.5 hours, or until the lamb is fork-tender and reaches an internal temperature of 190-200F (88-93C). The cooking time will vary based on the size of your lamb cut. Baste the lamb with pan juices every hour.

instructions.title: Prepare the Aromatic Ouzi Rice
instructions.text: While the lamb rests, in a separate large pot, melt ghee or butter over medium heat. Add the soaked and drained basmati rice, stirring for 2-3 minutes until lightly toasted. Pour in the remaining 4 cups of broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until all liquid is absorbed and rice is fluffy. Gently stir in the frozen peas and diced carrots (if using), then cover and let sit for 5 minutes.

instructions.title: Assemble the Ouzi
instructions.text: Once the lamb is rested, carefully remove it from the pot. Fluff the rice with a fork. You can either serve the lamb whole on top of a bed of rice, or shred the lamb meat and mix it gently into the rice. For a traditional presentation, mound the spiced rice onto a large serving platter.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Place the whole or shredded lamb on top of the rice. Drizzle with any remaining pan juices from the lamb. Generously sprinkle with toasted slivered almonds, pine nuts, and fresh chopped parsley or mint. Serve immediately with a side of yogurt or a simple cucumber salad for a complete Middle Eastern feast.

tips: For maximum flavor, marinate the lamb overnight. The longer it marinates, the deeper the spices will penetrate the meat.
Do not overcook the rice. Basmati rice should be fluffy and separate, not sticky. Ensure your broth is hot when added to the rice to maintain temperature and cooking consistency.
Allow the roasted lamb to rest for at least 15-20 minutes after removing it from the oven. This allows the juices to redistribute, resulting in a more tender and flavorful meat.
article:
Experience the ultimate Middle Eastern culinary masterpiece: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted. This majestic dish is more than just a meal; it's a celebration, a centerpiece that embodies hospitality and the rich flavors of the Levant. Imagine succulent, fall-off-the-bone lamb, infused with aromatic spices, often encasing a fragrant rice pilaf studded with nuts and dried fruits. Perfect for special occasions, family gatherings, or simply when you crave an unforgettable dining experience, mastering this Middle Eastern dinner recipe will elevate your culinary prowess and delight your guests. Preparing a whole slow-roasted Lamb Ouzi is a labor of love, but the reward is a dish so tender, so flavorful, it will become a cherished tradition in your home.

## What is Lamb Ouzi?

Lamb Ouzi, often spelled "Ozi" or "Awzi," is a grand Middle Eastern dish typically served at feasts, weddings, and important family gatherings. At its heart, it’s a whole leg or shoulder of lamb (sometimes even a whole young lamb) that is slow-roasted until incredibly tender. What truly distinguishes Ouzi, however, is the customary stuffing of spiced rice. This fragrant basmati rice is cooked with ground meat (often lamb or beef), onions, garlic, and a medley of warm spices like cinnamon, cardamom, allspice, and black pepper. It's often enriched with toasted nuts such as pine nuts, almonds, and pistachios, and sometimes dried fruits like raisins or apricots, adding layers of texture and sweetness. The lamb is marinated, sometimes even partially deboned and stuffed, then slow-cooked to perfection, allowing its natural juices to meld with the rice, creating a symphony of flavors. It’s a dish that commands attention, making it the perfect showstopper for any Middle Eastern dinner recipe collection.

## Ingredients for Lamb Ouzi

Crafting the perfect Lamb Ouzi Recipe requires a selection of fresh, high-quality ingredients. Here’s what you’ll need for a truly authentic experience:

For the Lamb:
*   1 whole bone-in lamb leg or shoulder (approx. 5-7 lbs / 2.5-3 kg)
*   1 cup plain full-fat yogurt
*   1/4 cup olive oil
*   6-8 cloves garlic, minced
*   Juice of 1 large lemon
*   2 tbsp Middle Eastern Seven Spice blend (or a mix of allspice, cinnamon, nutmeg, ginger, black pepper, cardamom, cloves)
*   1 tbsp ground cumin
*   1 tbsp ground coriander
*   1 tsp smoked paprika
*   1 tsp turmeric
*   Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste

For the Spiced Rice Stuffing:
*   2 cups basmati rice, rinsed until water runs clear
*   1 lb (450g) ground lamb or beef
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   4 cloves garlic, minced
*   1/4 cup pine nuts, lightly toasted
*   1/4 cup slivered almonds, lightly toasted
*   1/4 cup golden raisins (optional)
*   2 tbsp olive oil
*   1 tbsp Middle Eastern Seven Spice blend
*   1 tsp ground cinnamon
*   1/2 tsp ground cardamom
*   Salt and pepper to taste
*   3-4 cups chicken or beef broth

For Garnish:
*   Fresh parsley or mint, chopped
*   Extra toasted pine nuts and slivered almonds

## Step-by-Step Lamb Ouzi Instructions

Preparing this grand Lamb Ouzi Recipe is a journey, but a rewarding one. Follow these steps for a perfect whole slow-roasted lamb:

1.  **Prepare the Lamb:** Pat the lamb leg dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make several deep incisions all over the lamb to allow the marinade to penetrate. In a large bowl, whisk together yogurt, olive oil, minced garlic, lemon juice, seven spice, cumin, coriander, paprika, turmeric, salt, and pepper. Rub this marinade generously all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into the incisions. Place the lamb in a large roasting pan, cover tightly, and refrigerate for at least 8 hours, or preferably overnight, to allow the flavors to meld.

2.  **Prepare the Rice Stuffing:** While the lamb is roasting or just before, prepare your rice. Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and ground lamb/beef, breaking it up with a spoon, and cook until browned. Drain any excess fat. Stir in the seven spice, cinnamon, cardamom, salt, and pepper. Add the rinsed basmati rice, stirring to coat the grains with the spices and meat. Pour in the chicken or beef broth, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 10-12 minutes. The rice should be partially cooked (al dente). Remove from heat and gently stir in the toasted pine nuts, slivered almonds, and golden raisins (if using).

3.  **Stuff and Roast the Lamb:** Preheat your oven to 325°F (160°C). If you've deboned the lamb, carefully stuff the rice mixture into the cavity and tie it securely with butcher's twine. If using a bone-in leg, you can either create a pocket for some stuffing or simply place the rice around the lamb in the roasting pan later.
    Place the marinated lamb in a sturdy roasting pan. Add 1-2 cups of water or broth to the bottom of the pan to prevent drying and create steam. Cover the pan tightly with foil.
    Roast for 3.5 to 4.5 hours, or until the internal temperature reaches 145°F (63°C) for medium-rare, or 160°F (71°C) for medium. For fall-off-the-bone tenderness, continue roasting until the lamb reaches 190-200°F (88-93°C). Baste the lamb every hour or so with the pan juices.

4.  **Finish the Dish:** During the last hour of roasting, if you haven't already, add the remaining spiced rice mixture around the lamb in the pan, ensuring it’s covered by the lamb’s juices or a little extra broth. Remove the foil for the last 30-45 minutes to allow the lamb to brown and crisp up. If the lamb is browning too quickly, re-cover it.
    Once cooked, remove the Lamb Ouzi from the oven. Tent it loosely with foil and let it rest for at least 20-30 minutes before carving. This resting period is crucial for the juices to redistribute, ensuring a tender and moist result.

## Pro Tips for Perfect Slow Roasted Lamb

Achieving a truly spectacular whole slow-roasted Lamb Ouzi requires a few expert touches:

*   **Marinate Thoroughly:** Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the lamb sits in the yogurt-spice mixture, the more tender and flavorful it will become. Overnight is ideal.
*   **Low and Slow is Key:** Resist the urge to crank up the heat. Slow roasting at a lower temperature breaks down the tough connective tissues in the lamb, resulting in incredibly tender meat that practically melts in your mouth.
*   **Use a Meat Thermometer:** This is your best friend for perfect lamb. For fall-off-the-bone tenderness, aim for an internal temperature of 190-200°F (88-93°C) in the thickest part of the meat, avoiding the bone.
*   **Rest the Lamb:** This step is non-negotiable. Resting allows the muscle fibers to relax and reabsorb the juices, ensuring a succulent and moist carving experience.
*   **Baste for Moisture:** Regularly basting the lamb with its own pan juices helps to keep the exterior moist and adds another layer of flavor.
*   **Don't Overstuff:** If you're stuffing the lamb, ensure you don't pack the rice too tightly, as it will expand during cooking. Leave some room for expansion to avoid a dense, clumpy rice filling.

## Serving Suggestions for Lamb Ouzi

Lamb Ouzi is a feast in itself, but it truly shines when presented with traditional Middle Eastern accompaniments. Carve the succulent lamb and arrange it majestically on a large platter, surrounded by the fragrant spiced rice. Garnish generously with fresh chopped parsley or mint and extra toasted nuts like pine nuts and almonds for added crunch and visual appeal.

Classic sides that complement this rich dish include:
*   **Fresh Salads:** A vibrant Fattoush or Tabbouleh salad provides a refreshing counterpoint to the richness of the lamb.
*   **Yogurt Sauce:** A simple garlic-yogurt sauce (yogurt, minced garlic, a squeeze of lemon, and a pinch of salt) is excellent for drizzling over the lamb and rice.
*   **Pickles:** Assorted Middle Eastern pickles (like pickled cucumbers, turnips, or wild cucumbers) offer a tangy contrast.
*   **Warm Flatbreads:** Pita bread or other flatbreads are perfect for scooping up the delicious meat and rice.
*   **Roasted Vegetables:** Simple roasted vegetables like carrots, potatoes, or bell peppers can be cooked alongside the lamb or served separately.

## Variations and Substitutions for Ouzi

While the traditional Lamb Ouzi Recipe is a masterpiece, there are several ways to adapt it to your preferences or available ingredients:

*   **Different Cuts of Lamb:** If a whole leg or shoulder is too much, you can use a smaller boneless lamb roast or even individual lamb shanks. Adjust cooking times accordingly.
*   **Stuffing Variations:** For a vegetarian stuffing, omit the ground meat and increase the amount of nuts, dried fruits, and perhaps add some chickpeas or lentils. You can also experiment with different spice blends, adding a touch of baharat or even a hint of saffron to the rice.
*   **Spice Adjustments:** Feel free to adjust the spice levels to your liking. If you prefer a milder flavor, reduce the amount of seven spice. For more heat, add a pinch of chili flakes.
*   **Cooking Methods:** While oven roasting is traditional, you could also adapt the Lamb Ouzi Recipe for a slow cooker for smaller cuts, or even a smoker for a unique smoky flavor profile. If using a slow cooker, ensure the lamb is browned first for better flavor.
*   **Nut and Fruit Options:** If you don't have pine nuts, walnuts or cashews can be good substitutes. Dried cranberries or chopped dried apricots can replace raisins for a different sweetness.

No matter how you prepare it, this Lamb Ouzi Recipe is a testament to the warmth and generosity of Middle Eastern cuisine. It promises an unforgettable meal that will leave a lasting impression on all who gather around your table. Embark on this culinary adventure and bring the heart of the Middle East to your home.
